title: Doogh (Persian Yogurt Drink)
description: A refreshing, savory, and slightly effervescent Persian yogurt drink flavored with mint. The perfect thirst-quencher for hot days and rich meals.

instructions.text: In a large pitcher, vigorously whisk the plain yogurt until it is completely smooth and creamy, with no lumps remaining.
Whisk in the dried mint and salt until well combined.
Gradually pour in the cold club soda or water while stirring gently. Use club soda for a fizzy, traditional texture, or plain water for a flat version.
Taste and adjust salt or mint as needed. Add the ice cubes to the pitcher.
Serve immediately in tall glasses, garnished with a tiny sprinkle of crushed dried rose petals on top.

tips: For an authentic tartness, leave your plain yogurt out at room temperature for a few hours before making the drink to let it sour naturally.
storage_notes: Best enjoyed immediately if using club soda. If using flat water, it can be kept in the fridge for up to 3 days. Stir before pouring.
serve_with: Chelo Kebab, Joojeh Kebab, or any hearty Persian rice dish.

substitutions.note: Still water is also completely authentic, often called 'Doogh-e ba ghaaz' (with gas) vs 'bi ghaaz' (without gas).
Pouneh is a very traditional and sharp wild mint used in Iran.
